verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p23) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24 family. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is localized to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae, indicating a dual-localization pattern: a dominant Golgi/COPI/COPII vesicle trafficking role alongside a documented PM pool. Its role as a vesicular cargo receptor means it cycles between Golgi and the plasma membrane. The surface pool is real but the protein's dominant compartment is endomembrane (Golgi). This fits dual_localization: the protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the PM via secretory cycling, but the Golgi is its canonical home.
